original_abstract:
In the contemporary digital landscape, data has emerged as a critical organizational asset, necessitating robust oversight to ensure its integrity, security, and strategic value. *Data Governance: A Guide* provides a comprehensive exploration of the frameworks, methodologies, and best practices required to manage the availability, usability, and safety of institutional data. The book establishes a foundational understanding of the core pillars of data governance—quality, security, privacy, and management—while addressing the practical complexities of implementing these programs within diverse organizational structures.

Through a blend of theoretical principles and real-world case studies, this guide demystifies the roles of data stewards and governance bodies, the development of standardized policies, and the selection of supporting technologies. It further examines the impact of data governance on business value, regulatory compliance (such as GDPR and HIPAA), and customer trust. Special attention is given to the evolving technological frontier, including the integration of artificial intelligence, blockchain, and cloud governance. Beyond technical implementation, the author emphasizes the importance of fostering a data-centric culture through leadership and change management. By offering maturity models and performance metrics, this work equips professionals and researchers with the tools to navigate the current data-driven era and anticipate future shifts in the global data landscape.
humanized_abstract:
In today’s digital world, data has become a corporate asset that needs to be properly managed to ensure data accuracy, security, and that it is leveraged to the advantage of the organization. * Data Governance: A Guide* is a seminal work dedicated to exploring the rules, processes, best practices and analyses that ensure data quality, availability, trustworthiness and security. This book lays the groundwork for understanding the fundamental components of data governance including data quality, security, privacy and governance and how to effectively implement Data Governance programs within various types of organizations.

Applying theories and real-world best practices, the book bridges the knowledge gap for data stewards, data governance committees, data governance policies, standards and tools, thereby providing a clear understanding of how data governance can enhance business operations, drive revenue and adhere to statutory laws and regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA and impact customer confidence. Focusing on emerging trends and advances in technology such as Big Data, artificial intelligence, and blockchain, cloud governance and leadership and change management, the book discusses how to develop a data culture for organisations and helps data professionals and researchers evaluate and benchmark their data management practice using global benchmarking data and models. With the book readers will be fully equipped to not only thrive in this data age but to also prepare them for a changing global data landscape.
